articles.title: Mbappe v Haaland: Who is more important for their country?
articles.summary: Two of the greatest forwards in world football will go head-to-head at Boston Stadium on Friday, as France and Kylian Mbappe take on Norway and Erling Haaland.

articles.title: Is Serena brave to make Wimbledon singles bid at the age of 44?
articles.summary: Gamechanger, trailblazer, history-maker: Is Serena brave to make a singles comeback at Wimbledon after only playing two doubles matches?

articles.title: SWPL: Champions Hearts face Aberdeen, Celtic and Rangers have away tests
articles.summary: Scottish Women's Premier League champions Hearts will kick off the defence of their title at home to Aberdeen on Sunday August 16.

articles.title: Wolves edge past Dragons to close in on top spot
articles.summary: Highlights of the Super League match between Warrington Wolves and Catalans Dragons.

articles.title: 'Brilliant finish' - Pepe gets his second as Ivory Coast double their lead
articles.summary: Nicolas Pepe gets on the end of Ibrahima Sangare's "incisive pass" to score his second of the game, as Ivory Coast take a 2-0 lead against Curacao in their Group E match at Philadelphia Stadium.

articles.title: Man Utd's Onana set for Trabzonspor loan
articles.summary: Manchester United goalkeeper Andre Onana is close to securing a second loan spell at Turkish club Trabzonspor.

articles.title: Burnley and West Ham to meet on first Championship weekend
articles.summary: Relegated Burnley and West Ham will meet on the opening weekend of the Championship season on Sunday, 16 August.

articles.title: French footballer Kies, 21, dies from drowning
articles.summary: French footballer Kenzo Kies has died at the age of 21 after drowning in the Rhone river.

articles.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
articles.summary: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

articles.title: Tuchel's England are opposite to Southgate's - and built to beat top teams
articles.summary: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

highlights.title: Premier League highlights this week - Week Highlights

articles.title: Yoon opens Women's PGA Championship with record 63 as Hull, Woad sit well back
articles.summary: Ina Yoon matched the best score in the history of the Women's PGA Championship with a nine-under 63 to take a two-stroke lead over Karis Davidson on Thursday in the third major of the season.

articles.title: Championship fixtures: Your club-by-club guide
articles.summary: See your Championship club's full fixture list for the forthcoming 2026-27 season.

articles.title: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward World Cup Pride Match
articles.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.

articles.title: Beccacece goes from brink to World Cup history with Ecuador
articles.summary: Sebastian Beccacece was on the verge of losing his job as Ecuador head coach as the clock ticked down against Germany - now he has a last-32 match to look forward to.

articles.title: Turkey end World Cup on high with last-gasp win over USA
articles.summary: Turkey come out on top in the last minute of an end-to-end 3-2 win over a second string USA side, in their final Group D match at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.

articles.title: Japan and Sweden draw takes both into last 32
articles.summary: Daizen Maeda's smart finish to Japan's team goal is cancelled out by Anthony Elanga's long-range strike as Sweden fight back in a 1-1 draw that takes both sides through to the World Cup knockout stage...

articles.title: Brobbey on target again as Netherlands beat Tunisia to win group
articles.summary: Brian Brobbey scores for the second game in a row as Netherlands defeat Tunisia 3-1 to top Group F, setting up a match against Morocco in the last 32 at the World Cup.

articles.title: Hart explains how goalkeepers are struggling with World Cup ball
articles.summary: Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart answers your questions about the Trionda World Cup ball and how it might be affecting goalkeepers at the tournament.

articles.title: 'Oh my word!' - Elanga 'stunner' draws Sweden level
articles.summary: Anthony Elanga scores a brilliant curling effort from the edge of the area to draw Sweden level with Japan in their FIFA World Cup Group F match at Dallas Stadium.

articles.title: The World Cup 'Pride Match' - concern, complaints and the hope of 'unity'
articles.summary: When Seattle announced it would host a Pride Match during the 2026 World Cup, it was long before the draw had even been made.

articles.title: Ecuador fight back to upset Germany
articles.summary: Goals from Nilson Angulo and Gonzalo Plata help Ecuador come from behind to beat Germany 2-1 to qualify for the knockout stages of the World Cup, as one of the top eight third-placed teams.

articles.title: Plata's 'touch of gold' gives Ecuador lead against Germany
articles.summary: Gonzalo Plata reacts quickest to direct Kevin Rodriuguez' flick on over Manuel Neuer, to give Ecuador a 2-1 lead over Germany in their FIFA World Cup Group E match at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

articles.title: Germany goal should have been ruled out for 'endangering opponent'
articles.summary: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Joe Hart, Ellen White and Lucas Leiva explain why Alexander Pavlovic's high boot in the build-up to Leroy Sane's goal was an example of "endangering the opponent".

articles.title: Sane fires Germany ahead as Ecuador calls for foul waived away
articles.summary: Leroy Sane gives Germany a 1-0 lead against Ecuador in the second minute of the World Cup Group E match at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

articles.title: Is the World Cup ball making it hard for goalkeepers?
articles.summary: While some goalkeepers have produced heroic performances in the World Cup, others have struggled with shots from outside the area, so is there a problem with the ball?

articles.title: Belgium going out? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - final group games
articles.summary: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the final set of group games at the 2026 World Cup.
